title: GROUP_LEAVE leave notify via empty GROUP_DELIVER silently dropped
description: relay/src/ws.rs:2093-2103: GROUP_LEAVE sends GROUP_DELIVER with payload: Vec::new() to notify remaining members that someone left. cli/src/commands.rs:1742-1763 receives GROUP_DELIVER and: (1) if MLS is active, passes the empty payload to process_for_group which returns Err (cannot deserialize empty bytes as MLS message) → warn and continue, silently discarding the notification; (2) if MLS is not active, passes empty bytes to serde_json::from_slice which returns Err → warn('unrecognized message format') and continue. In both cases the leave notification is silently dropped. Group members are never shown that a member departed. The GROUP_DELIVER wire format conflates three semantics (join invite = group name bytes, leave = empty bytes, message = actual payload) with no discriminator field, making correct client-side dispatch impossible. Fix: add a discriminator field to GroupDeliverParams (e.g. kind: join|leave|message) or send a dedicated GROUP_LEFT notification method.
